reforms.description: Korea's government plans to significantly increase total R&D expenditure to 4 percent of GDP by the year 2000, shifting its industrial policy to provide incentives for increasing R&D expenditures and enhancing the competitiveness of its firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan to raise R&D expenditure has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant policy change aimed at boosting R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The shift of industrial policy from measures aimed at promoting specific products and industries to incentives to increase R&D expenditures has been beneficial.

reforms.description: The government implemented changes to the tax system aimed at promoting investment in research and development, including a reduction in the corporate tax rate and the introduction of new programmes to upgrade technology and ease financial burdens for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are designed to stimulate R&D investment and enhance productivity among SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax system changes and SME support programmes have been put into effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a major overhaul of the tax system and support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced new programmes to upgrade the technology and ease the financial burdens of SMEs.

reforms.description: The government has launched a series of sectoral R&D programmes, particularly in the aeronautics and space sector, and established the Small Business Administration to assist SMEs with start-up, financing, technological development, and marketing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to promote technological advancement and support the growth of innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The sectoral R&D programmes and the Small Business Administration have been established.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but narrower in scope compared to major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched a series of sectoral R&D programmes, most notably in the aeronautics and space sector.
